title: Al-Mada' in during the civil war
description: It was reported that when Abu al-Saj, Ismail b. Farashah and Yahya b. Hafs were given cloaks of honor in order to proceed to al-MadYin; they set up camp at Suq al-Thalatha'. On Sunday, the twentieth of Rabi' I (April 21, 865), Abu al-Saj`* let his infantry ride the mules, and he set off towards al-Mada'in, which had been the trench of the Sassanian Emperor."" He wrote asking for reinforcements, and five hundred infantry of the regular army were sent to him. He himself had set out at the head of three thousand horsemen and infantry.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 35
